skill.name: gws-drive
skill.description: Use the Google Workspace CLI to manage Google Drive files, folders, permissions, comments, revisions, and shared drives.
skill.summary: Manage Google Drive resources through authenticated gws CLI commands.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are primarily Markdown backticks, documentation links, wildcard text, and relative links rather than executable code. The skill is still medium risk because it directs an authenticated gws CLI to manage Google Drive resources, including uploads, downloads, deletes, permission changes, and shared drive operations.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Google Drive CLI Can Modify User Data
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill documents commands for creating, updating, deleting, sharing, uploading, downloading, and exporting Drive resources. This is expected behavior for the skill, but a mistaken or overbroad prompt could alter or expose user Drive data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed API methods clearly include write, delete, permission, upload, download, and export operations. The risk is inherent to the documented purpose, not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Through gws CLI
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs the agent to run gws drive and gws schema commands. The commands are legitimate and bounded to the Google Workspace CLI, but they still execute local binaries and rely on the user's authenticated account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit and semantically aligned with the skill. No shell interpolation or hidden payload was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Flagged Markdown Syntax as Code Risk
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The many Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings correspond to Markdown inline code, API method names, links, wildcard MIME text, or ordinary documentation. No Ruby code, weak cryptographic implementation, or reconnaissance command was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows Markdown documentation and gws CLI examples, not executable Ruby, cryptographic code, or system reconnaissance logic.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Are Not Path Traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The path traversal findings refer to relative links pointing to neighboring skill documentation files. They do not read arbitrary files or construct paths from user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited ../ references are Markdown links or prerequisite documentation references. No filesystem API call or user-controlled path handling is present.

low_findings.title: Google Documentation URLs Are Benign References
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URL findings are links to Google Drive API documentation. They do not perform network requests or exfiltrate data by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are documentation hyperlinks in Markdown. The reviewed file contains no HTTP client code or suspicious external endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Drive API Methods Documented
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill exposes legitimate Drive operations such as delete, update, permissions update, and revision delete. These operations should be confirmed with the user before execution in real accounts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The destructive methods are explicitly documented. They are not malicious, but they can cause data loss or unintended access changes.

dangerous_patterns.title: Drive Content Transfer Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents upload, download, export, and file content update operations. These can move user data between local storage and Google Drive when invoked.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited file methods include media creation, download, export, and content update. This creates a real data handling risk even though it is expected functionality.

content.user_title: Manage Google Drive from AI Workflows
content.value_statement: Google Drive administration often requires exact CLI syntax and Drive API resource names. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through authenticated gws commands for files, permissions, comments, revisions, and shared drives.
actual_capabilities: Lists Drive resources and method groups available through gws drive.
Guides use of gws schema before calling specific Drive API methods.
Supports file metadata, upload, download, export, copy, update, watch, and list workflows.
Covers comments, replies, approvals, access proposals, labels, revisions, and operations.
Documents shared drive and permission management commands for authenticated users.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI and the shared authentication skill to be available.
Does not grant Google Drive access without valid user credentials and scopes.
Does not replace user review for destructive operations such as delete or permission changes.
Large exports and uploads remain subject to Google Drive API limits.

use_cases.title: Audit Drive Sharing
use_cases.description: Review permissions on files or shared drives and prepare safer update commands.

use_cases.title: Organize Project Files
use_cases.description: Find files, copy documents, update metadata, and manage folders through guided CLI calls.

use_cases.title: Automate Drive Workflows
use_cases.description: Build repeatable command sequences for comments, revisions, labels, and change tracking.

prompt_templates.title: Find Drive Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws Drive skill to inspect the correct schema and list my recent Google Drive files matching a name or folder I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Review File Permissions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gws drive to inspect the permissions schema for a file, then prepare a command to list current access without changing anything.

prompt_templates.title: Update Shared Drive Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the shared drive update method, identify required parameters, and draft the safest gws command to update only the requested metadata fields.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Drive Migration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a Drive workflow that lists source files, validates schemas, copies selected files, checks permissions, and reports every command before execution.

output_examples.input: List files named quarterly report and exclude trashed files.
output_examples.output: The assistant inspects the files list schema, suggests a query using the name and trashed filters, and explains the fields to request.

output_examples.input: Check who can access this shared drive.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the permission listing workflow, asks for the drive ID, and prepares a read-only command for review.

output_examples.input: Export a Google Doc as a PDF.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks the export method, confirms the target MIME type, and prepares a download-safe command with the document ID.

best_practices: Run gws schema for every method before building params or json flags.
Use narrow fields and filters to reduce unnecessary Drive data exposure.
Confirm delete, update, sharing, upload, and download commands before execution.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad list commands when a specific file ID or query is available.
Do not change permissions without reviewing the target principal and role.
Do not download or export sensitive files unless the user explicitly requests it.

faq.question: Does this skill authenticate to Google Drive by itself?
faq.answer: No. It depends on the gws CLI and the shared Google Workspace authentication guidance.

faq.question: Can it upload and download files?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented Drive file methods include create, update, download, and export workflows.

faq.question: Can it manage shared drives?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers create, get, list, hide, unhide, and update methods for shared drives.

faq.question: Can it change file permissions?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents permission create, delete, get, list, and update methods, so changes need user confirmation.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for read-only audits?
faq.answer: Yes, when prompts restrict commands to list, get, and schema inspection methods.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The file is documentation, but the guided CLI can alter, share, upload, download, or delete real Drive data.
